title: AAA: sudo, PAM, and Polkit
obj_title: Summarize authorization, authentication, and accounting methods
goal: Configure sudo authorization, inspect PAM authentication stacks, and enable auditd accounting to record security events. Finish with a conceptual look at centralized identity via LDAP, Kerberos, and SSSD.
build: Install sudo, grant a user privileges, inspect PAM, read auth logs, and enable auditd rules.

steps.title: Install sudo and create a user
steps.code:
apt update && apt install -y sudo
useradd -m -s /bin/bash alice
echo "alice:Passw0rd!" | chpasswd
id alice
steps.explain: Creates user alice with a home directory and bash shell.

steps.title: Grant sudo via group and drop-in file
steps.code:
usermod -aG sudo alice
EDITOR=tee visudo -f /etc/sudoers.d/lab <<'EOF'
alice ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl status *, /usr/bin/apt update
EOF
chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/lab
visudo -c
sudo -l -U alice
steps.explain: A validated sudoers drop-in grants two NOPASSWD commands without editing the master file.

steps.title: Switch identity with sudo and su
steps.code:
su - alice -c 'sudo -n systemctl status ssh || true'
su - alice -c 'whoami; sudo -i whoami'
steps.explain: sudo -i opens a root login shell; su - becomes another user with a full login environment.

steps.title: Inspect PAM stacks
steps.code:
ls /etc/pam.d/
grep -vE '^\s*#|^$' /etc/pam.d/sshd
grep -vE '^\s*#|^$' /etc/pam.d/common-auth
apt show sssd 2>/dev/null | head -n 20
steps.explain: PAM chains auth/account/password/session modules; SSSD integrates Linux with LDAP/Kerberos/AD.

steps.title: Read auth logs via journald and rsyslog
steps.code:
apt install -y rsyslog
systemctl enable --now rsyslog
journalctl _COMM=sshd --no-pager | tail -n 20 || true
tail -n 20 /var/log/auth.log 2>/dev/null || journalctl -u ssh --no-pager | tail
logrotate -d /etc/logrotate.conf 2>&1 | tail -n 20
steps.explain: journalctl filters by process name; logrotate -d dry-runs rotation without changing files.

steps.title: Enable auditd and watch /etc/passwd
steps.code:
apt install -y auditd audispd-plugins
systemctl enable --now auditd
auditctl -w /etc/passwd -p wa -k passwd_changes
auditctl -l
echo "# test comment" >> /etc/passwd
ausearch -k passwd_changes | tail -n 20
steps.explain: The watch records write and attribute changes to /etc/passwd, queried by key with ausearch.

steps.title: Persistent audit rules and Polkit note
steps.code:
cat >/etc/audit/rules.d/lab.rules <<'EOF'
-w /etc/passwd -p wa -k passwd_changes
-w /etc/shadow -p wa -k shadow_changes
-w /etc/sudoers -p wa -k sudoers_changes
EOF
augenrules --load
auditctl -l
which pkexec && pkexec --version
steps.explain: Rules in /etc/audit/rules.d/ survive reboot; pkexec is the Polkit equivalent of sudo.

test: ausearch -k passwd_changes returns the recorded write event, confirming accounting is active.

title: Firewalls: ufw, firewalld, iptables, nftables, ipset
obj_title: Configure and implement firewalls on a Linux system
goal: Configure firewalls at every layer of the Linux netfilter stack, from the ufw wrapper down to iptables and nftables, and understand stateful inspection. Explore zones, rich rules, ipset, and SNAT/DNAT.
build: Configure ufw, firewalld zones, nftables, ipset, NAT rules, and a stateful conntrack pattern.

steps.title: Install and configure ufw
steps.code:
apt update && apt install -y ufw
ufw allow 22/tcp
ufw deny 23/tcp
yes | ufw enable
ufw status verbose
steps.explain: ufw is a friendly netfilter wrapper; verbose status shows defaults, logging, and rule order.

steps.title: Explore iptables backing ufw
steps.code:
iptables -L -n -v
iptables -t nat -L -n -v
steps.explain: ufw writes rules into the filter table ufw-* chains; the nat table stays empty until NAT rules are added.

steps.title: firewalld zones and rich rules in Rocky
steps.code:
apt install -y docker.io
systemctl start docker
docker run --rm --privileged -it rockylinux:9 bash -c '
  dnf install -y firewalld iproute && \
  firewall-offline-cmd --zone=public --add-service=https && \
  firewall-offline-cmd --zone=public --add-rich-rule="rule family=ipv4 source address=10.0.0.0/24 service name=ssh accept" && \
  firewall-offline-cmd --list-all --zone=public
'
steps.explain: firewall-offline-cmd configures zones without the daemon; rich rules add source-based ACLs.

steps.title: nftables: table, chain, and a rule
steps.code:
apt install -y nftables
nft add table inet lab
nft 'add chain inet lab input { type filter hook input priority 0 ; policy accept ; }'
nft add rule inet lab input tcp dport 23 drop
nft list ruleset
steps.explain: nftables is the modern netfilter front end; the inet family covers both IPv4 and IPv6.

steps.title: ipset for high-performance IP lists
steps.code:
apt install -y ipset
ipset create badguys hash:ip
ipset add badguys 192.0.2.10
ipset add badguys 198.51.100.5
ipset list badguys
iptables -I INPUT -m set --match-set badguys src -j DROP
steps.explain: ipset stores many addresses in an O(1) hash referenced by a single iptables rule.

steps.title: SNAT vs DNAT and masquerade
steps.code:
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1
iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o eth0 -s 10.10.0.0/24 -j MASQUERADE
iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -i eth0 -p tcp --dport 8080 -j DNAT --to-destination 10.10.0.50:80
iptables -t nat -L -n -v
steps.explain: SNAT/MASQUERADE rewrites source addresses outbound; DNAT rewrites destinations for port forwarding.

steps.title: Stateful inspection demo
steps.code:
iptables -A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -m conntrack --ctstate NEW -j ACCEPT
iptables -L INPUT -n -v
steps.explain: The canonical stateful pattern: allow return traffic, then permit only new SSH connections.

test: ufw status and nft list ruleset show the loaded rules; conntrack states drive stateful acceptance.

title: OS Hardening: Permissions, SELinux, SSH, Fail2ban
obj_title: Apply operating system hardening techniques on a Linux system
goal: Apply layered OS hardening from file permissions and immutability through SELinux labeling, then harden OpenSSH and add brute-force protection. Inventory SUID binaries and legacy cleartext services.
build: Set special permission bits, ACLs, and immutability, demo SELinux, harden SSH, and run fail2ban.

steps.title: chmod, chown, special bits
steps.code:
useradd -m alice 2>/dev/null
mkdir -p /srv/lab && cd /srv/lab
cat >run.sh <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
id
EOF
chmod 750 run.sh
chmod u+s run.sh
chmod g+s .
chmod +t /tmp
chown root:alice run.sh
ls -l run.sh /tmp -d .
steps.explain: setuid runs a file as its owner, setgid on a dir inherits the group, sticky bit protects /tmp.

steps.title: umask, chattr, lsattr
steps.code:
umask 027
touch /srv/lab/newfile && ls -l /srv/lab/newfile
chattr +i /etc/resolv.conf
lsattr /etc/resolv.conf
chattr -i /etc/resolv.conf
steps.explain: umask 027 yields 640 files; chattr +i makes a file immutable even to root until cleared.

steps.title: POSIX ACLs
steps.code:
apt install -y acl
touch /tmp/file
setfacl -m u:alice:r /tmp/file
getfacl /tmp/file
ls -l /tmp/file
steps.explain: ACLs extend owner/group/other; the trailing + in ls -l flags extra ACL entries.

steps.title: SELinux demo inside Rocky
steps.code:
apt install -y docker.io && systemctl start docker
docker run --rm -it rockylinux:9 bash -c '
  dnf install -y policycoreutils selinux-policy-targeted policycoreutils-python-utils setools-console && \
  getenforce || echo "SELinux disabled in container"; \
  ls -Z /etc/passwd; \
  echo "Workflow: chcon -t httpd_sys_content_t /web; semanage fcontext -a ...; restorecon -Rv /web"; \
  echo "Denials: ausearch -m AVC | audit2allow -M mypol"
'
steps.explain: Ubuntu uses AppArmor, so SELinux labeling and audit2allow are shown in a Rocky container.

steps.title: Harden OpenSSH
steps.code:
apt install -y openssh-server
sed -i 's/^#\?PermitRootLogin.*/PermitRootLogin no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
sed -i 's/^#\?PasswordAuthentication.*/PasswordAuthentication no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
sshd -t && systemctl restart ssh
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -N '' -f /root/.ssh/id_ed25519
mkdir -p /home/alice/.ssh
cat /root/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub >> /home/alice/.ssh/authorized_keys
chmod 700 /home/alice/.ssh && chmod 600 /home/alice/.ssh/authorized_keys
steps.explain: Disabling root login and password auth forces key-based access; sshd -t validates before restart.

steps.title: fail2ban
steps.code:
apt install -y fail2ban
cat >/etc/fail2ban/jail.local <<'EOF'
[sshd]
enabled = true
maxretry = 5
bantime = 600
EOF
systemctl enable --now fail2ban
fail2ban-client status
fail2ban-client status sshd
steps.explain: fail2ban tails auth logs and bans IPs exceeding maxretry failures within findtime.

steps.title: Hunt SUID binaries and review legacy services
steps.code:
find / -perm -4000 -type f 2>/dev/null | head -n 20
dpkg -l | grep -Ei 'telnet|tftp|vsftpd|rsh' || echo "Clean: no legacy daemons installed"
systemctl list-unit-files | grep -Ei 'telnet|tftp|ftp' || true
steps.explain: SUID binaries are escalation targets; cleartext telnet/FTP/TFTP should be replaced with SSH/SFTP/HTTPS.

test: fail2ban-client status sshd shows the active jail and sshd -t confirms the hardened config is valid.

title: Account Hardening
obj_title: Explain account hardening techniques and best practices
goal: Harden user accounts through password quality and aging policies, brute-force lockouts, and restricted or non-interactive shells. Explore TOTP MFA and Have I Been Pwned k-anonymity lookups.
build: Tune login.defs, enforce pwquality, set aging with chage, add pam_faillock, and lock down shells.

steps.title: login.defs and a test user
steps.code:
apt update && apt install -y libpam-pwquality
useradd -m -s /bin/bash alice
echo "alice:Passw0rd!" | chpasswd
sed -i 's/^PASS_MAX_DAYS.*/PASS_MAX_DAYS   90/' /etc/login.defs
sed -i 's/^PASS_MIN_DAYS.*/PASS_MIN_DAYS   1/' /etc/login.defs
grep -E '^PASS_' /etc/login.defs
steps.explain: /etc/login.defs sets system-wide defaults applied to newly created accounts.

steps.title: Password quality with pwquality
steps.code:
cat >/etc/security/pwquality.conf <<'EOF'
minlen = 14
dcredit = -1
ucredit = -1
lcredit = -1
ocredit = -1
retry = 3
EOF
pwscore <<<"short"
pwscore <<<"Tr0ub4dor&3LongerPhrase!"
steps.explain: Negative credits require each character class; pwscore rates a candidate password 0-100.

steps.title: Password aging and lock/unlock
steps.code:
chage -M 90 -m 1 -W 7 alice
chage -l alice
passwd -l alice
passwd -S alice
passwd -u alice
passwd -S alice
steps.explain: chage -l lists aging; passwd -l locks by prefixing ! to the hash, -u unlocks.

steps.title: pam_faillock lockout
steps.code:
cat >/etc/security/faillock.conf <<'EOF'
deny = 5
unlock_time = 900
fail_interval = 900
EOF
grep -l pam_faillock /etc/pam.d/* 2>/dev/null || echo "Add pam_faillock preauth/authfail lines to common-auth"
faillock --user alice
steps.explain: pam_faillock (successor to pam_tally2) locks after 5 failures; --reset clears the counter.

steps.title: Service accounts with nologin and rbash
steps.code:
useradd -r -s /usr/sbin/nologin svcacct
grep svcacct /etc/passwd
ln -sf /bin/bash /usr/local/bin/rbash
useradd -m -s /usr/local/bin/rbash boxed
echo 'PATH=/usr/local/rbin' >> /home/boxed/.bashrc
mkdir -p /usr/local/rbin && ln -sf /bin/ls /usr/local/rbin/ls
su - boxed -c 'cd /tmp 2>&1 || echo "rbash blocks cd"'
steps.explain: nologin blocks interactive daemon logins; rbash prevents cd, PATH reassignment, and redirection.

steps.title: MFA with Google Authenticator (walkthrough)
steps.code:
apt install -y libpam-google-authenticator
echo "Run as the target user, not root:"
echo "  google-authenticator -t -d -f -r 3 -R 30 -W"
echo "Then add 'auth required pam_google_authenticator.so' to /etc/pam.d/sshd"
echo "and set ChallengeResponseAuthentication yes in /etc/ssh/sshd_config."
steps.explain: Interactive setup prints a QR code; the TOTP secret is stored in ~/.google_authenticator.

steps.title: Pwned Passwords k-anonymity lookup
steps.code:
apt install -y curl
PW='password123'
HASH=$(printf "%s" "$PW" | sha1sum | awk '{print toupper($1)}')
PREFIX=${HASH:0:5}; SUFFIX=${HASH:5}
curl -s "https://api.pwnedpasswords.com/range/$PREFIX" | grep -i "^$SUFFIX" \
  && echo "BREACHED" || echo "Not found"
steps.explain: Only the first 5 SHA-1 chars are sent; the client filters returned suffixes locally.

test: chage -l alice and passwd -S alice confirm aging and lock state; the HIBP query flags breached passwords.

title: Cryptography: GPG, LUKS, OpenSSL, WireGuard
obj_title: Explain cryptographic concepts and technologies in a Linux environment
goal: Practice core Linux cryptography: GPG key generation and signing, LUKS2 disk encryption, OpenSSL certificates, hashing and HMAC, and WireGuard keys. Learn to identify weak TLS versions and ciphers.
build: Generate GPG keys, encrypt/sign, build a LUKS2 volume, create an OpenSSL cert, and make WireGuard keys.

steps.title: GPG key generation in batch mode
steps.code:
apt update && apt install -y gnupg
mkdir -p /root/.gnupg && chmod 700 /root/.gnupg
cat >/tmp/gpgparams <<'EOF'
%no-protection
Key-Type: RSA
Key-Length: 4096
Name-Real: Lab
Name-Email: lab@example.com
Expire-Date: 0
%commit
EOF
gpg --batch --generate-key /tmp/gpgparams
gpg --list-keys
steps.explain: %no-protection skips passphrase prompts for unattended use; Expire-Date: 0 is non-expiring.

steps.title: Encrypt, decrypt, and detached-sign
steps.code:
echo "top secret payload" > /tmp/msg.txt
gpg --yes --trust-model always -r lab@example.com -e /tmp/msg.txt
gpg --yes -d /tmp/msg.txt.gpg
gpg --yes --detach-sign /tmp/msg.txt
gpg --verify /tmp/msg.txt.sig /tmp/msg.txt
steps.explain: A detached .sig verifies integrity without altering the original file.

steps.title: LUKS2 on a loopback device
steps.code:
apt install -y cryptsetup
dd if=/dev/zero of=/tmp/luks.img bs=1M count=64
LOOP=$(losetup -f --show /tmp/luks.img)
echo -n "Passw0rd!" | cryptsetup luksFormat --type luks2 "$LOOP" -
echo -n "Passw0rd!" | cryptsetup open "$LOOP" labvol -
mkfs.ext4 /dev/mapper/labvol
mkdir -p /mnt/lab && mount /dev/mapper/labvol /mnt/lab
echo "data at rest" > /mnt/lab/file
umount /mnt/lab && cryptsetup close labvol && losetup -d "$LOOP"
steps.explain: LUKS2 carries key slots and Argon2 params; closing returns the file to opaque ciphertext.

steps.title: OpenSSL keys and self-signed certificate
steps.code:
apt install -y openssl
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/key.pem 2048
openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes -keyout /tmp/srv.key \
  -out /tmp/cert.pem -days 365 -subj "/CN=lab.example.com"
openssl x509 -in /tmp/cert.pem -noout -text | head -n 25
steps.explain: req -x509 produces a self-signed cert in one step; x509 -text decodes issuer, subject, and validity.

steps.title: Hashing and HMAC
steps.code:
sha256sum /tmp/msg.txt
openssl dgst -sha256 /tmp/msg.txt
openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac "secret-key" /tmp/msg.txt
steps.explain: A hash gives integrity only; an HMAC binds the hash to a shared secret for authentication too.

steps.title: WireGuard key pair and sample config
steps.code:
apt install -y wireguard-tools
umask 077
wg genkey | tee /etc/wireguard/priv | wg pubkey > /etc/wireguard/pub
cat >/etc/wireguard/wg0.conf <<EOF
[Interface]
PrivateKey = $(cat /etc/wireguard/priv)
Address = 10.20.0.1/24
ListenPort = 51820

[Peer]
PublicKey = REPLACE_WITH_PEER_PUBKEY
AllowedIPs = 10.20.0.2/32
EOF
cat /etc/wireguard/wg0.conf
steps.explain: WireGuard uses Curve25519 keys; wg-quick up wg0 would raise the tunnel given a peer.

steps.title: TLS version and cipher inspection
steps.code:
echo | openssl s_client -connect example.com:443 -tls1_2 2>/dev/null \
  | openssl x509 -noout -issuer -subject -dates
echo "TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are deprecated; prefer TLS 1.2 and 1.3."
echo "Avoid RC4, 3DES, MD5, and any 'EXPORT' cipher suites."
steps.explain: s_client prints the negotiated cert chain; disable legacy versions and weak ciphers.

test: gpg --verify confirms the signature and gpg -d recovers plaintext; the LUKS volume mounts only with the passphrase.

title: Compliance, Auditing, and File Integrity
obj_title: Explain the importance of compliance and audit procedures
goal: Establish file-integrity monitoring and run compliance and audit tooling to detect tampering, rootkits, and misconfigurations. Cover secure deletion, login banners, and OpenSCAP/CIS benchmarks.
build: Baseline files with AIDE, scan with rkhunter/lynis/nmap, verify packages, and set banners.

steps.title: AIDE baseline and tamper check
steps.code:
apt update && apt install -y aide
aideinit -y -f 2>/dev/null || aide --init
mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new /var/lib/aide/aide.db
echo "tampered" >> /etc/hostname
aide --check | head -n 40
steps.explain: aide --init builds the cryptographic baseline; --check reports any change against it.

steps.title: Rootkit hunting with rkhunter
steps.code:
apt install -y rkhunter
rkhunter --update || true
rkhunter --propupd -q
rkhunter --check --sk --rwo
steps.explain: --sk skips prompts, --rwo shows only warnings, --propupd resyncs the property DB.

steps.title: Package verification: debsums and rpm -V
steps.code:
apt install -y debsums
debsums -c | head -n 20 || echo "No changed files detected"
apt install -y docker.io && systemctl start docker
docker run --rm rockylinux:9 bash -c 'rpm -V coreutils || echo "rpm -V flags S/M/5/D/L/U/G/T/P"'
steps.explain: debsums -c reports modified Debian-packaged files; rpm -V is the RPM equivalent.

steps.title: System audit with lynis
steps.code:
apt install -y lynis
lynis audit system --quick --no-colors 2>&1 | tail -n 40
ls /var/log/lynis*.log
steps.explain: lynis runs hundreds of hardening checks and outputs a hardening index and suggestions.

steps.title: nmap service detection
steps.code:
apt install -y nmap openssh-server
systemctl start ssh
nmap -sV -p22,80 localhost
steps.explain: -sV probes service banners to identify versions, the first step of vulnerability assessment.

steps.title: Secure deletion and wipe
steps.code:
echo "very sensitive" > /tmp/secret.txt
shred -vn 3 -u /tmp/secret.txt
ls /tmp/secret.txt 2>/dev/null || echo "Shredded"
dd if=/dev/zero of=/tmp/wipe.img bs=1M count=10
LOOP=$(losetup -f --show /tmp/wipe.img)
dd if=/dev/urandom of="$LOOP" bs=1M count=10 status=progress
losetup -d "$LOOP" && rm -f /tmp/wipe.img
steps.explain: shred overwrites 3 times and unlinks; on SSDs use full-disk encryption due to wear leveling.

steps.title: Banners and OpenSCAP / CIS pointers
steps.code:
echo "Authorized use only. Activity is monitored." > /etc/issue
cp /etc/issue /etc/issue.net
echo "Welcome to Lab Host" > /etc/motd
apt install -y libopenscap8 || apt install -y openscap-scanner
oscap --version | head -n 5
echo "CIS Benchmarks: https://www.cisecurity.org/cis-benchmarks"
steps.explain: /etc/issue shows on TTY, issue.net on network logins, motd after login; OpenSCAP evaluates SCAP/XCCDF content.

test: aide --check flags the tampered /etc/hostname, proving the integrity baseline detects unauthorized change.
